strings.title: Ensign College MAT 107 · Unofficial student practice
strings.disclaimer: Unofficial student practice tool for Ensign College MAT 107, based on a real course assignment. Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to Ensign College or The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints. Made by a student for personal study. Built with Cursor, hosted on Cloudflare Workers, and versioned on GitHub.
strings.topics_blurb: Mastery = 10 correct with no hints per topic. Wrong answers never lower mastery. Hinted corrects (or skip after a hint) lower the counter by 1 — including after you’ve mastered a topic. Hints never reveal the answer — only how to work. Grade credit if correct: Hint 1 −25%, Hint 2 −50%, Hint 3 −75%. After a miss you can still enter the right answer once for 5% credit (not mastery).
strings.mode_finalboss_ready: Face the Gadianton Robber · save Zarahemla
strings.mode_finalboss_cleared: Zarahemla is safe ✓ · ready for the assessment
strings.boss_start: A Gadianton Robber threatens Zarahemla. Answer one unaided question from each topic — no hints. One miss, and he escapes on that subject (it drops to 9/10).
strings.boss_start_practice: Practice boss fight: one unaided question from each topic — no hints. One miss ends the run. Master every topic at 10/10 for the true defense of Zarahemla.
strings.boss_ok: The robber staggers · {current}/{total}. Press on for Zarahemla.
strings.boss_fail: Zarahemla is in danger. The Gadianton Robber slips away on {topic} — that topic drops to {progress}. Rebuild it to 10/10, then face him again.
strings.boss_fail_practice: Practice run failed on {topic}. No mastery penalty this time — master all topics at 10/10 when you’re ready for the real fight.
strings.boss_win: The Gadianton Robber is defeated — Zarahemla is saved. You’re ready for the assessment.
strings.boss_win_again: Zarahemla stands again. Still looking assessment-ready.
strings.boss_win_practice: Practice victory! Master every topic at 10/10, then face him again to truly save Zarahemla and earn assessment-ready status.
strings.boss_invite_mastered: Every topic is mastered at 10/10. A Gadianton Robber threatens Zarahemla — fight him now?
strings.hint_overview.generic: Overview: read the question carefully, list what you know, choose the right formula or rule, substitute values, then compute and round only at the end.
strings.hint_overview.conversions: Overview: write the given amount, multiply by a conversion fraction so unwanted units cancel, then simplify.
strings.hint_overview.formulas: Overview: identify the shape/measure (perimeter, area, volume, etc.), recall its formula, then match symbols to the letters in the prompt.
strings.hint_overview.perimeter_area: Overview: decide if you need distance around (perimeter) or space inside (area). Break composite figures into familiar shapes, label every length, then add/subtract carefully.
strings.hint_overview.volume: Overview: find the base area first (or use the solid’s formula), multiply by height when needed, and use π = 3.14 when the problem says so.
strings.hint_overview.pythagorean: Overview: identify the right triangle’s legs and hypotenuse. For length use √(a²+b²) or √(c²−a²); for slope use rise/run.
strings.hint_overview.scale_rates: Overview: write a proportion from the scale (map:real), or for carpet decide roll vs sq-yd pricing, solve for the missing amount, and keep units consistent.
strings.hint_overview.scaling: Overview: area scales with the square of the scale factor; volume scales with the cube. Compare totals after scaling each piece.
strings.hint_overview.stats_center: Overview: sort the data if needed. Mean = sum÷n, median = middle, mode = most frequent, range = max−min.
strings.hint_overview.stats_spread: Overview: use the range rule of thumb s ≈ range/4, or compare ranges/approximate SDs across data sets.
strings.hint_overview.z_scores: Overview: z = (x − mean) / SD. Percentiles come from the area to the left of z on a standard normal.
strings.hint_overview.distributions: Overview: name the shape from the long tail (skew) or use the empirical rule (68% / 95% / 99.7%) for roughly normal data.
strings.hint_overview.literacy: Overview: ask who was surveyed, how large the sample was, and whether the graph or claim could mislead.
strings.clarify_intro: Walkthrough — use this to unlock the method. The final answer is still yours to finish.
strings.clarify_step_setup: Set up (do not jump to the final boxed answer yet):
strings.clarify_step_finish: Finish: plug into your calculator carefully, convert any fraction/exact display to decimal, and round only as the question requires.
strings.clarify_fallback: Re-read the prompt, write the known values, pick a formula, substitute, then compute. If that still feels unclear, request AI help next.
strings.clarify_ai_edge: This quiz can't go deeper automatically. You're on Microsoft Edge — open Copilot and paste the practice question for a step-by-step explanation ({url}).
strings.clarify_ai_chrome: This quiz can't go deeper automatically. You're on Google Chrome — open Gemini and paste the practice question for a step-by-step explanation ({url}).
strings.clarify_ai_other: This quiz can't go deeper automatically. Try Microsoft Copilot or Google Gemini and paste the practice question for a step-by-step explanation.
strings.clarify_ai_prompt_template:
I'm practicing MAT107. Explain how to solve this step by step without just giving the final number first:

{prompt}
strings.btn_remix_aria: Remix question — same style with new numbers
strings.notes_placeholder: Jot working, formulas, or a data set here…
strings.notes_sort_asc_title: Sort numbers ascending (uses selection if any)
strings.notes_sort_desc_title: Sort numbers descending (uses selection if any)
strings.notes_sum_title: Append count and sum of numbers
strings.mastery_blurb: Need 10 unaided corrects to master a topic. Wrong answers never change mastery. Hinted corrects (or skip after a hint) drop the counter by 1. 5% retries never add mastery.
strings.reset_confirm: Reset all progress stored in this browser?
strings.load_fail: Failed to load quiz scripts. Open index.html from the project folder (keep the js/, css/, and lang/ folders beside it).
strings.feedback_wrong_retry: Not quite. Enter the correct answer for 5% credit (does not count toward mastery).
strings.feedback_retry_ok: Correct on retry · 5% credit. (Not counted toward mastery.)
strings.pi_note: Use π = 3.14. Round to the nearest hundredth.
strings.calc_generic:
How to enter it (you finish and round):
TI-36X Pro / Casio: type the setup left → right using × ÷ + − and parentheses.
Squares: press x² after the number. Square roots: TI press 2nd x² ; Casio press √.
Decimals: TI press ◄► ; Casio press S⇔D. Round to the place asked—usually hundredths. The hint never shows the final answer.
strings.calc_generic_ti:
TI-36X Pro — how to enter it (you finish and round):
Type the setup left → right using × ÷ + − and parentheses.
Squares: press x² after the number. Square roots: press 2nd x².
Decimals: press ◄►. Round to the place asked—usually hundredths. The hint never shows the final answer.
strings.calc_generic_casio:
Casio fx-115ES/991ES — how to enter it (you finish and round):
Type the setup left → right using × ÷ + − and parentheses.
Squares: press x² after the number. Square roots: press √.
Decimals: press S⇔D. Round to the place asked—usually hundredths. The hint never shows the final answer.
strings.calc_panel_ti:
TI-36X Pro — enter this (you finish and round):
{steps}
strings.calc_panel_casio:
Casio fx-115ES/991ES — enter this (you finish and round):
{steps}
strings.flash.target_form: Target form: {left} = …  (fill the right-hand side from memory)
strings.calc_tip_default: Do not use the π key when a problem says π = 3.14 — type 3.14. Convert exact/fraction displays with ◄► (TI-36X Pro) or S⇔D (Casio), then round yourself to the place asked (usually hundredths). Hints never show the final answer.
strings.card.sq_p.front: Perimeter of a square with side s
strings.card.sq_a.front: Area of a square with side s
strings.card.rect_p.front: Perimeter of a rectangle with length L and width W
strings.card.rect_a.front: Area of a rectangle with length L and width W
strings.card.tri_p.front: Perimeter of a triangle with sides a, b, and c
strings.card.tri_a.front: Area of a triangle with base b and height h
strings.card.circ_c.front: Circumference of a circle with radius r
strings.card.circ_c.hint: Also C = πd with diameter d.
strings.card.circ_a.front: Area of a circle with radius r
strings.card.cube.front: Volume of a cube with side s
strings.card.sphere.front: Volume of a sphere with radius r
strings.card.cyl.front: Volume of a cylinder with radius r and height h
strings.card.cyl.hint: Area of base circle times height.
strings.card.pyth.front: Pythagorean Theorem (legs a, b; hypotenuse c)
strings.card.pyth.hint: Sum of the squares of the legs equals the square of the hypotenuse.
strings.flash.recognize:
Formula flashcard — which is correct for:
{front}?
strings.flash.match_hint: Match the shape name to its formula. Correct pattern starts like: {left} = …
strings.q.feet_in_yard: How many feet are in one yard? (Useful when measuring the cultural-hall volleyball court.)
strings.q.yd_to_ft: A Primary streamer needs to be {yards} yards long for the cultural-hall wall. How many feet is that?
strings.q.yd_to_ft.v3: A fabric remnant is {yards} yards. What is that length in feet?
strings.h.yd_to_ft: 1 yard = 3 feet, so multiply yards by 3.
strings.q.sqft_sqyd: How many square feet are in one square yard? (Relief Society floor plans require this trivia.)
strings.h.sqft_sqyd: A square yard is 3 ft × 3 ft
strings.q.sqyd_to_sqft: Sister Jensen ordered {sqYd} square yards of linoleum for a classroom. How many square feet is that?
strings.q.sqyd_to_sqft.v2: Convert {sqYd} square yards to square feet.
strings.q.sqyd_to_sqft.v3: A floor section is {sqYd} sq yd. How many square feet?
strings.h.sqyd_to_sqft: 1 square yard = 9 square feet, so multiply by 9.
strings.q.cuft_cuyd: How many cubic feet are in one cubic yard? (Food-storage wheat math, anyone?)
strings.h.cuft_cuyd: A cubic yard is 3 ft × 3 ft × 3 ft
strings.q.cuyd_to_cuft: The stake bought {cuYd} cubic yards of mulch for the meetinghouse landscaping. How many cubic feet is that?
strings.q.cuyd_to_cuft.v2: Convert {cuYd} cubic yards to cubic feet.
strings.q.cuyd_to_cuft.v3: A delivery of {cuYd} cu yd — how many cubic feet?
strings.h.cuyd_to_cuft: 1 cubic yard = 27 cubic feet, so multiply by 27.
strings.q.form_sq_p: What is the formula for the perimeter of a square with side length s?
strings.q.form_sq_p.v2: A square has side s. Which expression gives its perimeter?
strings.q.form_sq_p.v3: Choose the perimeter formula for a square (side s).
strings.q.form_sq_a: What is the formula for the area of a square with side length s?
strings.q.form_sq_a.v2: A square has side s. Which expression gives its area?
strings.q.form_sq_a.v3: Choose the area formula for a square (side s).
strings.q.form_rect_p: What is the formula for the perimeter of a rectangle with length L and width W?
strings.q.form_rect_p.v2: Rectangle with length L and width W — what is the perimeter formula?
strings.q.form_rect_p.v3: Which formula finds the distance around a rectangle (L by W)?
strings.q.form_rect_a: What is the formula for the area of a rectangle with length L and width W?
strings.q.form_rect_a.v2: Rectangle L by W — which expression is the area?
strings.q.form_rect_a.v3: Choose the area formula for a rectangle with length L and width W.
strings.q.form_tri_a: What is the formula for the area of a triangle with base b and height h?
strings.q.form_tri_a.v2: Triangle with base b and height h — area formula?
strings.q.form_tri_a.v3: Which expression gives the area of a triangle (base b, height h)?
strings.q.form_circ_c: What is the formula for the circumference of a circle with radius r?
strings.q.form_circ_c.v2: Circle radius r — which formula is the circumference?
strings.q.form_circ_c.v3: Choose the circumference formula for a circle with radius r.
strings.q.form_circ_a: What is the formula for the area of a circle with radius r?
strings.q.form_circ_a.v2: Circle radius r — which formula is the area?
strings.q.form_circ_a.v3: Choose the area formula for a circle with radius r.
strings.q.form_cyl: What is the formula for the volume of a cylinder with radius r and height h?
strings.q.form_cyl.v2: Cylinder: radius r, height h. Which volume formula?
strings.q.form_cyl.v3: Choose the volume formula for a cylinder (r, h).
strings.q.form_cube: What is the formula for the volume of a cube with side s?
strings.q.form_cube.v2: Cube with side s — volume formula?
strings.q.form_cube.v3: Which expression is the volume of a cube with side s?
strings.q.form_sphere: What is the formula for the volume of a sphere with radius r?
strings.q.form_sphere.v2: Sphere radius r — which volume formula?
strings.q.form_sphere.v3: Choose the volume formula for a sphere with radius r.
strings.q.form_pyth: Which equation is the Pythagorean Theorem for a right triangle with legs a, b and hypotenuse c?
strings.q.form_pyth.v2: Right triangle legs a, b and hypotenuse c — which Pythagorean equation is correct?
strings.q.form_pyth.v3: Choose the Pythagorean Theorem relating a, b, and c.
strings.q.form_tri_p: What is the formula for the perimeter of a triangle with sides a, b, and c?
strings.q.form_tri_p.v2: Triangle sides a, b, c — perimeter formula?
strings.q.form_tri_p.v3: Which expression gives the perimeter of a triangle with sides a, b, and c?
strings.q.dim_concept.v2: About dimensions: which claim is true?
strings.q.dim_concept.v3: Pick the correct statement about perimeter, area, and volume.
strings.c.dim_a: Perimeter is 1D, area is 2D, and volume is 3D
strings.c.dim_b: Perimeter is 2D, area is 1D, and volume is 3D
strings.c.dim_c: Perimeter is 1D, area is 3D, and volume is 2D
strings.c.dim_d: Perimeter, area, and volume are all 2D
strings.q.rect_p: The cultural hall needs painter’s tape around a rectangular court marking that measures {L} ft by {W} ft (yes, the same court where the stake Young Men still think they’re NBA-bound). What is its perimeter in feet?
strings.q.rect_a: Sister Jensen is measuring linoleum for a rectangular Primary classroom {L} ft by {W} ft. (She muttered, “And it came to pass… we need more area.”) What is the floor area in square feet?
strings.h.rect_p: P = 2L + 2W (or 2(L+W)). On TI-36X Pro / Casio enter the expression, then =.
strings.h.rect_a: A = L × W. On TI-36X Pro / Casio enter length × width, then =.
strings.q.tri_area: A youth pioneer-trek map shows a triangular meadow with base {b} units and height {h} units—basically the only flat place that isn’t a dust storm. What is its area? Round to the nearest hundredth if needed.
strings.h.tri_area: A = ½bh. Enter half × base × height on your calculator.
strings.q.circ_c: The ward has a circular green Jell-O “ring of fire” mold with radius {r} inches (a culinary classic at every potluck from Provo to Pocatello). What is its circumference? {pi_note}
strings.q.circ_a: A circular sacrament-meeting clock face (the one that miraculously slows during talks) has radius {r}. What is its area? {pi_note}
strings.h.circ_c: C = 2πr. Type 3.14 for π (do not use the π key—answers are keyed to 3.14).
strings.h.circ_a: A = πr². Type 3.14 for π. Use x² for the square.
strings.q.lshape_a: The bishopric office remodel left an L-shaped floor plan that fits in a {W} × {H} rectangle, with a {cutW} × {cutH} rectangle cut from one corner for the elusive “emergency cookies” closet. What is the area of the L-shape?
strings.q.lshape_p: Nephi’s handcart storage shed (hypothetically) is an L-shape fitting in a {W} × {H} rectangle with a {cutW} × {cutH} corner notch removed—because even prophets need weird floor plans. What is the outer perimeter of the L-shape?
strings.h.lshape_a: Area of L = area of big rectangle − area of cut piece
strings.h.lshape_p: Walking the outside edge: the cut adds and removes edges that cancel — P = 2(W+H)
strings.q.combo_trs_a: Find the area of the composite figure shown below (right triangle + rectangle + semicircle). Use the labeled lengths: triangle base {a}, rectangle width {b}, semicircle radius {r}. The shared height equals the diameter (2r). {pi_note}
strings.q.combo_trs_a.v2: What is the total area of the shaded figure? It is a right triangle (base {a}), a rectangle (width {b}), and a semicircle (radius {r}) joined side-by-side. Height = diameter = 2r. {pi_note}
strings.q.combo_trs_a.v3: The figure below is made of a triangle, a rectangle, and a semicircle. Labeled: base {a}, width {b}, radius {r}. Find the area of the whole region. {pi_note}
strings.q.combo_trs_p: Find the perimeter of the composite figure shown below (right triangle + rectangle + semicircle). Use the labeled lengths: triangle base {a}, rectangle width {b}, semicircle radius {r}. Walk only the outside edge — do not count shared internal sides. Height equals the diameter (2r). {pi_note}
strings.q.combo_trs_p.v2: What is the outer perimeter of the shaded figure? Dimensions on the diagram: triangle base {a}, rectangle width {b}, radius {r} (diameter = height = 2r). Round to the nearest hundredth. {pi_note}
strings.q.combo_trs_p.v3: Find the distance around the outside of the triangle–rectangle–semicircle figure. Labeled lengths are {a}, {b}, and radius {r}. Omit internal edges (including the diameter). {pi_note}
strings.h.combo_trs_a: Area = triangle + rectangle + semicircle. Height h = 2r. A = ½ah + bh + ½πr².
strings.h.combo_trs_p: Perimeter uses only outside edges: triangle hypotenuse + bottom (a+b) + top of rectangle (b) + semicircle arc (πr). Do not add the shared diameter.
strings.q.fence_need: Brother Larsen wants to fence the ward community garden—{ft1} feet {in1} inches by {ft2} feet {in2} inches—so the zucchini stop “going on missions” into Sister Clark’s yard. How many feet of fencing do you need to go around it? Round to the nearest hundredth.
strings.q.fence_roll: Ammon is fencing a garden plot {ft1}' {in1}" by {ft2}' {in2}" (smaller than King Lamoni’s fields, thankfully). Fencing comes in 50-ft or 100-ft rolls at the Deseret Industries hardware aisle. Which roll do you need?
strings.h.fence: Convert inches to feet, then P = 2(L+W). Enter inches÷12 on the calculator.
strings.h.fence_roll: Convert dimensions to feet, find the perimeter P = 2(L+W), then choose the smallest roll that is at least P.
strings.q.soup: Relief Society is canning a legendary cylinder of funeral potatoes. The base is {d} inches across (diameter) and the can is {h} inches high. (If Nephi could build a ship, you can find a volume.) What is the volume? {pi_note}
strings.h.soup: V = πr²h, and r = diameter/2. Type 3.14 for π (not the π key).
strings.q.cube_vol: The Young Men found a cubic storage box with side length {s} inches (for the eternally missing volleyball). What is its volume?
strings.q.cube_vol.v2: A cube has side {s} inches. What is its volume?
strings.q.cube_vol.v3: Cube volume: side {s}. Compute V = s³.
strings.h.cube_vol: V = s³. Multiply side × side × side (or use x³).
strings.q.sphere_vol: A spherical Primary punch bowl has radius {r} inches. What is its volume? {pi_note}
strings.q.sphere_vol.v2: Sphere radius {r}. What is the volume? {pi_note}
strings.h.sphere_vol: V = (4/3)πr³. Type 3.14 for π (not the π key). Use parentheses: (4÷3)×3.14×r³.
strings.q.drive_slope: The stake-center driveway rises {rise} ft over a horizontal distance of {run} ft—steeper than a new missionary’s learning curve. What is the slope? Write as a simplified ratio rise:run or as a fraction rise/run.
strings.q.drive_len: Lehi’s family, if they had asphalt, might have used a driveway that rises {rise} ft over a horizontal distance of {run} ft. What is the slant length of that driveway? Round to the nearest hundredth. (Hint from Alma: have faith—and use √(a² + b²).)
strings.h.drive_slope: slope = rise / run (vertical change over horizontal change)
strings.h.drive_len: Use Pythagorean Theorem: c = √(a² + b²). Square root is 2nd x² on TI-36X Pro; √ on Casio.
strings.q.tv: Your family just scored a {diagonal}-inch TV that is {width} inches wide—perfect for General Conference (and for arguing over who sits where). How tall is it? Round to the nearest tenth. (TV size is the diagonal, not the spiritual “bigness” of the talks.)
strings.h.tv: Diagonal is hypotenuse: height = √(diagonal² − width²). Round to the nearest tenth.
strings.q.gal_l: You’ve got a {gallons}-gallon food-storage jug of punch for the ward picnic (the pink kind that always looks vaguely fluorescent). How many {bottleL}-liter bottles will it take to fill it? (1 gallon = 3.785 liters). Round to the nearest hundredth. “And it came to pass” that someone bought metric bottles.
strings.q.gal_l.v2: A {gallons}-gallon water cooler for trek needs emptying into {bottleL}-liter bottles. How many bottles? (1 gal = 3.785 L). Round to the nearest hundredth.
strings.q.gal_l.v3: You bought {gallons} gallons of paint for the scout room. How many {bottleL}-liter cans is that equivalent to? (1 gal = 3.785 L). Round to hundredths.
strings.q.gal_l.v4: A food-storage drum holds {gallons} gallons of wheat-free… wait, milk for the breakfast. How many {bottleL}-liter bottles fill it? (1 gal = 3.785 L). Round to the nearest hundredth.
strings.q.gal_l.v5: Convert {gallons} gallons to {bottleL}-liter bottles (1 gal = 3.785 L). Round to the nearest hundredth.
strings.q.gal_l.v6: The cultural-hall mop bucket recipe calls for {gallons} gallons of cleaner concentrate. How many {bottleL}-liter bottles is that? (1 gal = 3.785 L). Round to hundredths.
strings.h.gal_l: Convert gallons → liters, then divide by bottle size.
strings.q.map_to_real: A youth pioneer-trek map uses a scale of 1 inch : {scaleFt} feet. The rest stop (where they rediscover the joy of socks) is {inches} inches away on the map. How many feet down the trail is it?
strings.q.real_to_map: A ward “Book of Mormon lands” activity map has scale 1 inch : {scaleFt} feet. A landmark labeled “Zarahemla? (maybe)” is {feet} feet down the trail. How many inches away is it on the map? Round to the nearest hundredth if needed.
strings.h.map_to_real: Multiply map inches by the scale factor (feet per inch).
strings.q.carpet_roll: The Primary room is {L} ft × {W} ft and needs new carpet—preferably something that can survive grape juice and “Head, Shoulders, Knees, and Toes.” Carpet comes in a roll {rollW} ft wide at ${cost} per linear foot (installed). How much does this choice cost? Round to the nearest cent.
strings.q.carpet_yd: The cultural hall’s quiet-side classroom is {L} ft × {W} ft. Carpet costs ${cost} per square yard (installed)—cheaper than building a ship like Nephi, still pricey. How much does this choice cost? Round to the nearest cent. (9 sq ft = 1 sq yd)
strings.q.carpet_cmp: Relief Society is comparing carpet options for a {L}'×{W}' room (the one with the mysterious “temporary” craft closet from 1998). Option A: {rollW}'-wide roll at ${costLin}/linear ft. Option B: ${costYd}/sq yd. Which is cheaper?
strings.h.carpet_roll: Roll carpet is sold by the linear foot at a fixed roll width. Orient the roll so its width covers one room side if it fits: if roll width ≥ length, linear feet = width; if roll width ≥ width, linear feet = length; otherwise you need several strips (ceil of room length ÷ roll width) each as long as the other side. Cost = linear feet × price per linear foot.
strings.h.carpet_yd: First find floor area in sq ft (L × W). Convert to square yards: divide by 9 (because 9 sq ft = 1 sq yd). Then multiply by the price per square yard.
strings.h.carpet_cmp: Figure the total dollar cost both ways (roll: linear feet × $/ft; sq yd: (L×W÷9) × $/sq yd), then choose the smaller total. Unit prices alone can mislead.
strings.q.pizza_cmp: Mutual night pizza debate: which has more pizza area—two {small}-inch personal pizzas or one {large}-inch pizza? (Think feeding the multitude, but with cheese. {pi_note})
strings.q.pizza_double: What happens to the area of a ward-pizza when you double the radius? (And it came to pizza…)
strings.h.pizza_cmp: Area = πr². Doubling diameter (or radius) multiplies area by 4.
strings.h.pizza_double: A = πr², so if r → 2r then A → π(2r)² = 4πr²
strings.q.ball_double: Primary Activity Day has a ball holding {base} cubic inches of air. If they somehow get a ball with twice the radius (like a basketball that ate too much funeral potatoes), how much air would it hold?
strings.q.ball_half: Young Men brought a ball that holds {base} cubic inches of air. A smaller ball with half the radius somehow shows up at Saturday cultural-hall basketball (please don’t tell the bishop how many windows they’ve almost hit). How much air would the smaller ball hold?
strings.h.ball_double: Volume of a sphere scales with r³, so 2³ = 8 times the volume.
strings.h.ball_half: Volume scales with r³, so (1/2)³ = 1/8 of the volume.
strings.q.mean: Seminary Scripture Mastery practice scores for the week: {ds}. Find the mean. Round to the nearest hundredth if needed. (No, Brother Jensen, “spiritual GPA” is not a real thing.)
strings.q.median: Young Women activity attendance counts: {ds}. Find the median (the “middle” value after sorting—like finding the middle seat in a pew).
strings.q.mode: Primary singing-time song choices (coded as numbers): {ds}. Find the mode—the song that got requested most. (Hint: it was probably “I Am a Child of God.”)
strings.q.range: Trek snack counts for each family: {ds}. Find the range (max − min). Pioneer lesson: extremes happen.
strings.h.mean: Mean = sum of values ÷ number of values. Add first, then divide.
strings.h.median: Sort the data; median is the middle value (or average of two middle values)
strings.h.mode: Mode = value that appears most often
strings.q.best_outliers: Which measure of center is usually best when data has extreme outliers (like that one ward who brought 47 pans of funeral potatoes)?
strings.q.best_cat: Which measure of center is best for favorite hymns / favorite-color type (categorical) data?
strings.q.best_sym: Which measure of center uses every numeric value and fits roughly symmetric data well (like fairly even Mutual attendance)?
strings.h.best_outliers: The median is resistant to outliers; the mean is pulled by extremes.
strings.h.best_cat: Mode is the most frequent category.
strings.h.best_sym: The mean is the balancing point of all values.
strings.q.range_rule: Use the range rule of thumb to approximate the standard deviation of {ds}. Round to the nearest hundredth.
strings.h.range_rule: Range rule of thumb: s ≈ range / 4.
strings.q.compare_sd: {which}: {ds}. Approximate the standard deviation using the range rule of thumb.
strings.h.compare_which: Larger approximate SD (range/4) means more variation
strings.q.sd_tf: True or False: Data points must be exactly 1, 2, or 3 standard deviations above or below the mean.
strings.h.sd_tf: Z-scores can be any real number — data can sit anywhere relative to the mean.
strings.q.zscore: A seminary quiz has mean {mean} and standard deviation {sd}. What is the z-score for a score of {score}? Round to the nearest hundredth. (Seek learning by study and also by… z.)
strings.h.zscore: z = (x − mean) / standard deviation. Enter carefully with parentheses.
strings.q.pct_from_z: Using the standard normal (Z) table shown with this question, about what percentile is a data point that is {z} standard deviations from the mean? Enter the percentile as a number (e.g. 84 for the 84th percentile). Round to the nearest whole number.
strings.h.pct_from_z: Use the z-score / percentile table shown with this question. Find your z in the left column; the percentile is listed beside it. Round to the nearest whole number if asked.
strings.q.z_from_pct: Using the z-score / percentile table shown, what z-score corresponds to percentile {pct}?
strings.h.z_from_pct: Scan the Percentile column on the table shown until you find {pct} (or the closest listed value), then read the matching z-score.
strings.z_table_caption: Standard normal table — lookup z-score ↔ percentile
strings.emp_chart_show: Show normal curve / empirical rule chart
strings.emp_chart_caption: Normal distribution — percentage of data in each standard-deviation band
strings.emp_chart_aria: Normal curve showing empirical rule percentages
strings.q.skew: A data distribution is skewed right (tail to the right), like a rare “super-long” meeting that pulls the average up. Which is typically true?
strings.c.skew_d: The mode always equals the range
strings.h.skew: Right skew pulls the mean toward the long right tail.
strings.q.emp_1sd: Ages of adults in a (very hypothetical) stake directory are roughly normal with mean {mean} years and SD {sd} years. About what percentage are between {lo} and {hi}? (Don’t say “all of them left the potluck early.”)
strings.q.emp_2sd: Years of church membership for adults in a ward are roughly normal with mean {mean} and SD {sd}. About what percentage fall between {lo} and {hi}?
strings.q.emp_3sd: Ages of Gospel Doctrine class members are roughly normal with mean {mean} years and SD {sd} years. About what percentage are older than {cut} (the rare “I’ve read the footnotes in Isaiah” club)?
strings.q.emp_below: Missionary ages in a (made-up, PG) data set are roughly normal with mean {mean} and SD {sd}. About what percentage are younger than {cut}?
strings.h.emp_1sd: Empirical rule: ≈68% within 1 SD of the mean
strings.h.emp_2sd: Empirical rule: ≈95% within 2 SD of the mean
strings.h.emp_3sd: ≈99.7% within 3 SD, so about 0.3% outside total → ~0.15% in each tail
strings.h.emp_below: Below 1 SD left of mean ≈ half of the 32% outside the middle 68% → ~16%
strings.q.literacy: “Seek learning, even by study and also by faith” (D&C 88:118)—which practice best helps you use statistics for good decision-making?
strings.c.lit_a: Check the source, sample, and whether graphs/summaries could be misleading
strings.c.lit_b: Always trust a graph if it looks professional
strings.c.lit_c: Use only the mean and ignore the rest of the data
strings.h.literacy: Good questions: Who was surveyed? How big was the sample? What's being compared? Are axes truncated?
strings.notecard_disclaimer: Study template only — testing center usually requires a handwritten card.
strings.load_confirm: Replace current progress with this JSON file?
strings.progress_load_fail: Could not load that progress file. Use a MAT107 progress JSON export.
strings.q.skew_right: A histogram piles most values on the left with a long tail stretching to the right. How would you describe the distribution?
strings.q.skew_left: A histogram piles most values on the right with a long tail stretching to the left. How would you describe the distribution?
strings.q.skew_uniform: Looking at a weather-report style bar chart where daily values stay in a narrow band with no long tail, how would you describe it?
strings.q.skew_effect_follow: What does this do to mean, median, and mode?
strings.h.skew_right_effect: Right-skewed data usually pulls the mean to the right of the median (mean > median > mode, roughly).
strings.h.skew_left_effect: Left-skewed data usually pulls the mean left of the median (mean < median).
strings.h.skew_uniform_effect: Mean, median, and mode tend to be close together.
strings.h.skew_shape: Skew is named for the long tail direction.
strings.c.skew_wrong_a: Mean, median, and mode are always equal no matter the shape
strings.c.skew_wrong_b: The mode always equals the range
strings.c.skew_wrong_c: Skewness only affects the standard deviation, never center measures
strings.q.feet_in_yard.v2: One yard equals how many feet? (Handy before laying out chairs for stake conference.)
strings.q.feet_in_yard.v3: Convert: 1 yard → ? feet. Cultural-hall measuring crew needs the number.
strings.q.sqft_sqyd.v2: How many square feet make one square yard? (Floor-plan math for the meetinghouse remodel.)
strings.q.sqft_sqyd.v3: A square yard covers how many square feet?
strings.q.cuft_cuyd.v2: How many cubic feet are in a cubic yard? (Food-storage pantry volume check.)
strings.q.cuft_cuyd.v3: Convert 1 cubic yard into cubic feet.
strings.q.rect_p.v2: Tape off a {L} ft by {W} ft rectangle on the cultural-hall floor. What perimeter of tape do you need?
strings.q.rect_p.v3: A rectangular activity zone measures {L} ft × {W} ft. Find its perimeter in feet.
strings.q.rect_a.v2: A rectangular classroom is {L} ft by {W} ft. How many square feet of flooring are needed?
strings.q.rect_a.v3: Find the area (sq ft) of a {L} ft × {W} ft rectangle in the Primary area.
strings.q.tri_area.v2: A triangular plot on a trek map has base {b} and height {h}. What is its area?
strings.q.tri_area.v3: Triangle area time: base {b}, height {h}. Round to the nearest hundredth if needed.
strings.q.circ_c.v2: A circular Jell-O mold has radius {r} inches. What is its circumference? {pi_note}
strings.q.circ_c.v3: Find the circumference of a circle with radius {r}. {pi_note}
strings.q.circ_a.v2: A round clock face has radius {r}. What is its area? {pi_note}
strings.q.lshape_a.v2: An L-shaped room fits in a {W} × {H} rectangle with a {cutW} × {cutH} corner cut out. What is the L’s area?
strings.q.lshape_a.v3: Big rectangle {W} by {H}, remove a {cutW} by {cutH} corner. Area of the L-shape?
strings.q.lshape_p.v2: An L-shape sits in a {W} × {H} rectangle with a {cutW} × {cutH} notch removed. What is the outer perimeter?
strings.q.lshape_p.v3: Find the outer perimeter of an L that fits in {W}×{H} with a {cutW}×{cutH} cutout.
strings.q.fence_need.v2: Fence a garden {ft1} ft {in1} in by {ft2} ft {in2} in. How many feet of fencing for the perimeter? Round to the nearest hundredth.
strings.q.fence_need.v3: Plot size: {ft1}' {in1}" by {ft2}' {in2}". Length of fencing around it (feet, hundredths)?
strings.q.fence_roll.v2: Garden fence needed for {ft1}' {in1}" by {ft2}' {in2}". Rolls come in 50-ft or 100-ft. Which roll do you buy?
strings.q.fence_roll.v3: Perimeter fencing for {ft1}' {in1}" × {ft2}' {in2}". Choose the 50-foot or 100-foot roll.
strings.q.soup.v2: A cylindrical can has diameter {d} in and height {h} in. What is its volume? {pi_note}
strings.q.soup.v3: Cylinder volume: diameter {d} inches, height {h} inches. {pi_note}
strings.q.drive_slope.v2: A driveway rises {rise} ft over {run} ft horizontal. What is the slope (rise:run or rise/run, simplified)?
strings.q.drive_slope.v3: Slope check: rise {rise} ft, run {run} ft. Write a simplified ratio or fraction.
strings.q.drive_len.v2: Rise {rise} ft, run {run} ft. What is the slant length of the driveway? Round to the nearest hundredth.
strings.q.drive_len.v3: Find the hypotenuse for legs {rise} and {run}. Round to hundredths.
strings.q.tv.v2: A TV is labeled {diagonal} inches (diagonal) and is {width} inches wide. How tall is it? Round to the nearest tenth.
strings.q.tv.v3: Diagonal {diagonal}", width {width}". Find the height to the nearest tenth.
strings.q.map_to_real.v2: Map scale 1 in : {scaleFt} ft. A trail is {inches} inches on the map. How many real feet is that?
strings.q.map_to_real.v3: Scale: 1" = {scaleFt} ft. Map distance {inches}". Real distance in feet?
strings.q.real_to_map.v2: Scale 1 in : {scaleFt} ft. A landmark is {feet} feet away for real. How many inches on the map? Round if needed.
strings.q.real_to_map.v3: Real distance {feet} ft; scale 1" : {scaleFt} ft. Map length in inches?
strings.q.carpet_roll.v2: Room {L} ft × {W} ft. Carpet roll is {rollW} ft wide at ${cost}/linear ft installed. Total cost (nearest cent)?
strings.q.carpet_roll.v3: Cover {L}'×{W}' with a {rollW}'-wide roll at ${cost} per linear foot. Cost to the nearest cent?
strings.q.carpet_yd.v2: Room {L} ft × {W} ft. Carpet is ${cost} per square yard installed. Cost to the nearest cent? (9 sq ft = 1 sq yd)
strings.q.carpet_yd.v3: Floor {L}' by {W}' at ${cost}/sq yd. Total installed cost (nearest cent)?
strings.q.carpet_cmp.v2: Room {L}'×{W}'. Option A: {rollW}' roll at ${costLin}/linear ft. Option B: ${costYd}/sq yd. Which is cheaper?
strings.q.carpet_cmp.v3: Compare costs for a {L}'×{W}' room — roll ({rollW}' @ ${costLin}/ft) vs sq-yd pricing (${costYd}). Cheaper option?
strings.q.pizza_cmp.v2: More pizza area: two {small}-inch pizzas or one {large}-inch pizza? {pi_note}
strings.q.pizza_cmp.v3: Compare areas: 2×({small}" radius pizzas) vs 1×({large}" pizza). Which wins? {pi_note}
strings.q.pizza_double.v2: If you double a pizza’s radius, what happens to its area?
strings.q.pizza_double.v3: Pizza radius doubles. How does the area change?
strings.q.ball_double.v2: A ball holds {base} cubic inches of air. Double the radius—how much air then?
strings.q.ball_double.v3: Volume was {base} cu in. New ball has 2× the radius. New volume?
strings.q.ball_half.v2: A ball holds {base} cu in. Half the radius—how much air in the smaller ball?
strings.q.ball_half.v3: Volume {base} cu in; radius → ½. New volume?
strings.q.mean.v2: Data: {ds}. Find the mean. Round to the nearest hundredth if needed.
strings.q.median.v3: Find the middle value of {ds} after sorting.
strings.q.mode.v3: Which value appears most often in {ds}?
strings.q.range_rule.v2: Approximate the SD of {ds} with the range rule of thumb. Round to hundredths.
strings.q.range_rule.v3: Using s ≈ range/4, estimate the standard deviation of {ds}.
strings.q.zscore.v2: Mean {mean}, SD {sd}. Z-score for x = {score}? Round to hundredths.
strings.q.zscore.v3: Find z for score {score} when μ={mean} and σ={sd}. Round to the nearest hundredth.
strings.q.emp_1sd.v2: Roughly normal: mean {mean}, SD {sd}. About what % lie between {lo} and {hi}?
strings.q.emp_1sd.v3: Empirical rule: μ={mean}, σ={sd}. Percent between {lo} and {hi}?
strings.q.emp_2sd.v2: Normal-ish data: mean {mean}, SD {sd}. About what % fall between {lo} and {hi}?
strings.q.emp_2sd.v3: ≈95% rule check: μ={mean}, σ={sd}. Percent from {lo} to {hi}?
strings.q.emp_3sd.v2: Mean {mean}, SD {sd} (approx. normal). About what % are older/greater than {cut}?
strings.q.emp_3sd.v3: Tail question: μ={mean}, σ={sd}. Percent above {cut}?
strings.q.emp_below.v2: Approx. normal ages: mean {mean}, SD {sd}. About what % are younger than {cut}?
strings.q.literacy.v2: Which habit best supports wise use of statistics?
strings.q.literacy.v3: Best practice for reading studies and graphs?
strings.scripture_quote: “And I said: Lord, whither shall I go that I may find ore to molten, that I may make tools to construct the ship after the manner which thou hast shown unto me?”
strings.q.feet_in_yard.boss: How many feet are in one yard? (Captain Moroni needs the conversion before pacing the walls of Zarahemla against the Gadianton Robbers.)
strings.q.yd_to_ft.boss: Helaman’s scouts stretch a banner {yards} yards long along Zarahemla’s outer wall. How many feet is that?
strings.q.sqft_sqyd.boss: How many square feet are in one square yard? (Vital when reclaiming floor space after a Gadianton raid on Zarahemla.)
strings.q.sqyd_to_sqft.boss: The Nephites reclaim {sqYd} square yards of plaza stone in Zarahemla after Gadianton sabotage. How many square feet is that? (9 sq ft = 1 sq yd)
strings.q.cuft_cuyd.boss: How many cubic feet are in one cubic yard? (Grain stores for a siege of Zarahemla demand this conversion.)
strings.q.cuyd_to_cuft.boss: Captain Moroni orders {cuYd} cubic yards of earth for earthworks around Zarahemla. How many cubic feet is that?
strings.q.dim_concept.boss: Which statement is correct? (Answer wisely—Gadianton spies twist length, area, and volume to confuse Zarahemla’s builders.)
strings.q.form_sq_p.boss: What is the formula for the perimeter of a square with side length s? (Moroni’s square watch-tower plans depend on it.)
strings.q.form_sq_a.boss: What is the formula for the area of a square with side length s? (Needed for the square plazas of Zarahemla.)
strings.q.form_rect_p.boss: What is the formula for the perimeter of a rectangle with length L and width W? (Fence the rectangular barracks before the Gadiantons strike.)
strings.q.form_rect_a.boss: What is the formula for the area of a rectangle with length L and width W? (Supply-floor math for Helaman’s camp.)
strings.q.form_tri_p.boss: What is the formula for the perimeter of a triangle with sides a, b, and c? (Triangular signal-fires near Bountiful need the outer path.)
strings.q.form_tri_a.boss: What is the formula for the area of a triangle with base b and height h? (Triangular lookouts over the land of Zarahemla.)
strings.q.form_circ_c.boss: What is the formula for the circumference of a circle with radius r? (Circular watch-rings around the city’s approaches.)
strings.q.form_circ_a.boss: What is the formula for the area of a circle with radius r? (Circular muster grounds for the stripling warriors.)
strings.q.form_cube.boss: What is the formula for the volume of a cube with side s? (Cubic supply chests for Zarahemla’s defenders.)
strings.q.form_sphere.boss: What is the formula for the volume of a sphere with radius r? (Spherical signal stones used by Nephite scouts.)
strings.q.form_cyl.boss: What is the formula for the volume of a cylinder with radius r and height h? (Cylindrical water cisterns under siege.)
strings.q.form_pyth.boss: Which equation is the Pythagorean Theorem for a right triangle with legs a, b and hypotenuse c? (Right-angled ramparts—Moroni’s favorite geometry.)
strings.q.rect_p.boss: Gadianton Robbers probe a rectangular gate-yard in Zarahemla measuring {L} ft by {W} ft. Captain Moroni needs the fencing length around it. What is its perimeter in feet?
strings.q.rect_p.boss2: Helaman’s stripling warriors mark a rectangular muster field {L} ft by {W} ft outside Zarahemla’s walls. What is the perimeter in feet?
strings.q.rect_a.boss: Nephite quartermasters measure a rectangular armory floor in Zarahemla: {L} ft by {W} ft. What is the floor area in square feet?
strings.q.rect_a.boss2: After a Gadianton fire, builders restore a rectangular hall in Bountiful {L} ft by {W} ft. What is its area in square feet?
strings.q.tri_area.boss: Scouts map a triangular ridge overlooking Zarahemla with base {b} units and height {h} units—prime Gadianton ambush ground. What is its area? Round to the nearest hundredth if needed.
strings.q.circ_c.boss: A circular watch-tower ring around Zarahemla has radius {r} inches on the engineer’s model. What is its circumference? {pi_note}
strings.q.circ_a.boss: A circular signal drumhead used by Helaman’s runners has radius {r}. What is its area? {pi_note}
strings.q.combo_trs_a.boss: Find the area of the composite figure shown below (right triangle + rectangle + semicircle)—a fortified outpost plan near Zarahemla. Use the labeled lengths: triangle base {a}, rectangle width {b}, semicircle radius {r}. The shared height equals the diameter (2r). {pi_note}
strings.q.combo_trs_p.boss: Find the perimeter of the composite figure shown below (right triangle + rectangle + semicircle)—walk only the outer wall Helaman’s men must defend. Use the labeled lengths: triangle base {a}, rectangle width {b}, semicircle radius {r}. Do not count shared internal sides. Height equals the diameter (2r). {pi_note}
strings.q.lshape_a.boss: Gadianton saboteurs leave Zarahemla’s storehouse as an L-shaped floor fitting in a {W} × {H} rectangle, with a {cutW} × {cutH} rectangle cut from one corner. What is the area of the L-shape?
strings.q.lshape_p.boss: Moroni’s L-shaped barricade fits in a {W} × {H} rectangle with a {cutW} × {cutH} corner notch removed. What is the outer perimeter of the L-shape?
strings.q.fence_need.boss: Captain Moroni must fence a rectangular supply yard—{ft1} feet {in1} inches by {ft2} feet {in2} inches—before Gadianton raiders strike Zarahemla by night. How many feet of fencing do you need to go around it? Round to the nearest hundredth.
strings.q.fence_roll.boss: Ammon’s remnant fences a plot {ft1}' {in1}" by {ft2}' {in2}" near the borders. Fencing comes in 50-ft or 100-ft rolls. Which roll do you need?
strings.q.soup.boss: Under siege, Zarahemla stores grain in a cylindrical jar. The base is {d} inches across (diameter) and the jar is {h} inches high. What is the volume? {pi_note}
strings.q.cube_vol.boss: Stripling warriors find a cubic chest of side length {s} inches—sealed against Gadianton thieves. What is its volume?
strings.q.sphere_vol.boss: A spherical signal stone used by Nephite lookouts has radius {r} inches. What is its volume? {pi_note}
strings.q.drive_slope.boss: The approach road into Zarahemla rises {rise} ft over a horizontal distance of {run} ft—steep enough to slow Gadianton chargers. What is the slope? Write as a simplified ratio rise:run or as a fraction rise/run.
strings.q.drive_len.boss: A fortified ramp into Bountiful rises {rise} ft over a horizontal distance of {run} ft. What is the slant length of that ramp? Round to the nearest hundredth.
strings.q.tv.boss: Nephite mapmakers study a {diagonal}-inch rectangular battle chart that is {width} inches wide (the Title of Liberty briefing board). How tall is it? Round to the nearest tenth.
strings.q.gal_l.boss: Zarahemla’s cistern holds {gallons} gallons of water for the siege. How many {bottleL}-liter skins will it take to fill it? (1 gallon = 3.785 liters). Round to the nearest hundredth.
strings.q.map_to_real.boss: Helaman’s campaign map uses a scale of 1 inch : {scaleFt} feet. A Gadianton camp is {inches} inches away on the map. How many feet down the trail is it?
strings.q.real_to_map.boss: A Nephite war map of the land of Zarahemla has scale 1 inch : {scaleFt} feet. A lookout post is {feet} feet down the trail. How many inches away is it on the map? Round to the nearest hundredth if needed.
strings.q.carpet_roll.boss: Rebuilders cover a {L} ft × {W} ft council chamber in Zarahemla after Gadianton arson. Flooring comes in a roll {rollW} ft wide at ${cost} per linear foot (installed). How much does this choice cost? Round to the nearest cent.
strings.q.carpet_yd.boss: A {L} ft × {W} ft hall in Bountiful needs new flooring after the raid. It costs ${cost} per square yard (installed). How much does this choice cost? Round to the nearest cent. (9 sq ft = 1 sq yd)
strings.q.carpet_cmp.boss: Zarahemla’s builders compare flooring for a {L}'×{W}' room. Option A: {rollW}'-wide roll at ${costLin}/linear ft. Option B: ${costYd}/sq yd. Which is cheaper?
strings.q.pizza_cmp.boss: After victory, the camp shares round cakes: which has more area—two {small}-inch cakes or one {large}-inch cake? {pi_note}
strings.q.pizza_double.boss: What happens to the area of a circular Nephite signal disc when you double the radius?
strings.q.ball_double.boss: A spherical signal stone holds {base} cubic inches of air. If the stripling warriors craft one with twice the radius, how much air would it hold?
strings.q.ball_half.boss: A spherical scout float holds {base} cubic inches of air. A smaller stone with half the radius—hidden for a quiet approach on Gadianton camps—how much air would it hold?
strings.q.mean.boss: Daily scout reports of Gadianton sightings near Zarahemla: {ds}. Find the mean. Round to the nearest hundredth if needed.
strings.q.mean.boss2: Stripling-warrior drill scores under Helaman: {ds}. Find the mean. Round to the nearest hundredth if needed.
strings.q.median.boss: Night-watch headcounts on Zarahemla’s walls: {ds}. Find the median (the middle value after sorting).
strings.q.mode.boss: Coded signal-fire choices from the watchtowers: {ds}. Find the mode—the code used most often.
strings.q.range.boss: Captured Gadianton supply counts by band: {ds}. Find the range (max − min).
strings.q.best_outliers.boss: Which measure of center is usually best when data has extreme outliers (like one night when Gadianton numbers spike wildly outside Zarahemla)?
strings.q.best_cat.boss: Which measure of center is best for favorite banner color / battle-cry type (categorical) data among Nephite companies?
strings.q.best_sym.boss: Which measure of center uses every numeric value and fits roughly symmetric data well (like fairly even watch rotations in Zarahemla)?
strings.q.range_rule.boss: Use the range rule of thumb to approximate the standard deviation of {ds} (Gadianton raid counts). Round to the nearest hundredth.
strings.q.compare_sd.boss: {which}: {ds}. Approximate the standard deviation using the range rule of thumb. (Compare Nephite muster tallies under pressure.)
strings.q.sd_tf.boss: True or False: Data points must be exactly 1, 2, or 3 standard deviations above or below the mean. (Even Moroni’s muster rolls can sit anywhere relative to the mean.)
strings.q.zscore.boss: A Zarahemla readiness drill has mean {mean} and standard deviation {sd}. What is the z-score for a score of {score}? Round to the nearest hundredth.
strings.q.zscore.boss2: Helaman’s archery scores have mean {mean} and standard deviation {sd}. What is the z-score for a score of {score}? Round to the nearest hundredth.
strings.q.pct_from_z.boss: Using the standard normal (Z) table shown with this question, about what percentile is a scout whose z-score is {z} among Zarahemla’s defenders? Enter the percentile as a number (e.g. 84 for the 84th percentile). Round to the nearest whole number.
strings.q.z_from_pct.boss: Using the z-score / percentile table shown, what z-score corresponds to percentile {pct} among Nephite readiness scores?
strings.q.skew_right.boss: A histogram of Gadianton raid sizes piles most values on the left with a long tail stretching to the right (a few massive assaults on Zarahemla). How would you describe the distribution?
strings.q.skew_left.boss: A histogram of Nephite watch durations piles most values on the right with a long tail stretching to the left (rare very short watches). How would you describe the distribution?
strings.q.skew_uniform.boss: Looking at daily grain rations in Bountiful that stay in a narrow band with no long tail, how would you describe the distribution?
strings.q.skew_effect_follow.boss: What does this do to mean, median, and mode?
strings.q.emp_1sd.boss: Ages of Nephite defenders in a (hypothetical) Zarahemla roster are roughly normal with mean {mean} years and SD {sd} years. About what percentage are between {lo} and {hi}?
strings.q.emp_1sd.boss2: Years of service among Helaman’s stripling warriors are roughly normal with mean {mean} and SD {sd}. About what percentage fall between {lo} and {hi}?
strings.q.emp_2sd.boss: Years since first watch assignment among adults in Bountiful are roughly normal with mean {mean} and SD {sd}. About what percentage fall between {lo} and {hi}?
strings.q.emp_3sd.boss: Ages of captains under Moroni are roughly normal with mean {mean} years and SD {sd} years. About what percentage are older than {cut}?
strings.q.emp_below.boss: Ages of new stripling recruits are roughly normal with mean {mean} and SD {sd}. About what percentage are younger than {cut}?
strings.q.literacy.boss: As you defend Zarahemla with both courage and sound judgment—which practice best helps you use statistics for good decision-making against Gadianton deception?
strings.card.sq_p.front.boss: Zarahemla square watch-tower: perimeter formula (side s)?
strings.card.sq_a.front.boss: Square plaza in Zarahemla: area formula (side s)?
strings.card.rect_p.front.boss: Rectangular barracks under Moroni: perimeter (L, W)?
strings.card.tri_p.front.boss: Triangular signal fire near Bountiful: perimeter (a, b, c)?
strings.card.tri_a.front.boss: Triangular ridge lookout: area (base b, height h)?
strings.card.cube.front.boss: Cubic supply chest vs. Gadianton thieves: volume (side s)?
strings.card.cyl.front.boss: Cylindrical cistern under siege: volume (r, h)?
strings.card.pyth.front.boss: Right-angled rampart (Moroni): Pythagorean relation for a, b, c?
